'''Ben Kavanagh''' (born 4 March 1988) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] former international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Kavanagh was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England.

==Career==
Kavanagh initially joined Widnes on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 2008. He scored two tries in 21 appearances for the Widnes club, and joined the club permanently at the end of the season, signing a three-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|title=Vikings confirm Kavanagh signing|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7633968.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=29 October 2013|date=24 September 2008}}</ref>

He went on to make 72 consecutive appearances for the club, and was the first player to be given a Super League contract by Widnes when they were granted a licence for the 2012 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Widnes Vikings give Ben Kavanagh Super League contract|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9456765.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=29 October 2013|date=13 April 2011}}</ref>

Kavanagh is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international, and was named in their squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] as a replacement for the injured [[Keith Galloway]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Scotland add Widnes prop Ben Kavanagh to World Cup squad|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/8938785|work=Sky Sports|publisher=BSkyB|access-date=29 October 2013|date=24 September 2013}}</ref>

On 7 May 2014, Kavanagh committed to playing for Widnes for two more years.<ref>{{cite web| url = http://superleague.co.uk/article/30155/kavanagh-commits-to-widnes| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20140508122415/http://superleague.co.uk/article/30155/kavanagh-commits-to-widnes| archive-date = 2014-05-08| title = Kavanagh commits to Widnes}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/9301178/super-league-ben-kavanagh-signs-new-contract-with-widnes-vikings|title=Super League: Ben Kavanagh signs new contract with Widnes Vikings|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

In October and November 2014, Kavanagh played in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] competition. He played in all of Scotland's games and scored a try in their fixture against France.

In October and November 2015, Kavanagh played in the [[2015 European Cup]] competition.

Kavanagh signed a two-year Deal with the [[Bradford Bulls]] following the 2015 season. Kavanagh featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Castleford Tigers]].

In January 2017, it was revealed that Kavanagh had signed a two-year deal to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

In 2019, he joined [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]].  In September 2020, he signed a one-year contract extension with Halifax.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/fax-duo-kavanagh-and-jones-sign-new-contracts-2970402/|title=Fax duo Kavanagh and Jones sign new contracts|website=www.halifaxcourier.co.uk}}</ref>

On 15 November 2024 he announced his retirement<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Nov 2024|title=Former Super League veteran makes retirement call after 17-year playing career|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-veteran-ben-kavanagh-makes-retirement-call}}</ref>
